codeReview.summary: The SettingsPage was already well-polished before this feature. The worker's commit (d914eb4) enhanced the test suite from 6 basic tests to 17 comprehensive tests covering all VAL-SET-001 and VAL-SET-002 assertions. The implementation correctly uses PageHeader, displays all 4 sections (General, Approvals, Email, Advanced) with read-only text rendering, includes a Lock icon notice banner, and has no form inputs or edit controls. The code is clean, follows project conventions (CSS variables, responsive grid patterns, shared components), and the tests are thorough.

issues.description: The mock for lucide-react uses a generic M = () => null stub. This works but means the Lock icon test only verifies text content, not that the Lock icon component is actually rendered. The read-only notice test checks for /platform administrators|managed by/ text but doesn't verify the Lock icon is visually adjacent. This is adequate for unit testing but means a refactor that removes the Lock import wouldn't be caught.

The 'displays dash placeholders for missing optional fields' test uses a minimalSettings object that only includes core AdminSettings fields (no smtpHost, smtpPort, etc.), but doesn't assert which specific fields show dashes. It only checks that at least one dash exists. A more precise test would verify specific fields like SMTP Host, SMTP Port, etc. each show '—'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted that the frontend-worker skill says to invoke frontend-skill for UI work, but in this case the SettingsPage was already fully polished and only needed test coverage enhancement. The worker chose not to invoke frontend-skill, which was a reasonable decision for a test-only change. This suggests the skill could benefit from clearer guidance on when frontend-skill invocation is unnecessary.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill says to invoke frontend-skill for UI work, but in this case the SettingsPage was already fully polished and only needed test coverage enhancement - invoking frontend-skill would have been unnecessary overhead for a test-only change.'

summary: PASS — The SettingsPage implementation is complete and correct. It loads data from GET /api/v1/admin/settings via adminApi.getSettings(), displays all 4 sections (General, Approvals, Email, Advanced) with properly populated read-only text values, shows a Lock icon read-only notice banner, uses the shared PageHeader component, and has zero form inputs or edit controls. The commit enhanced test coverage from 6 to 17 tests covering all VAL-SET-001 and VAL-SET-002 assertions including API call verification, section rendering, value population, loading/error states, retry functionality, dash placeholders for missing fields, read-only enforcement (no inputs/textarea/select, no save/edit buttons, no updateSettings calls, no toggle switches), and Lock notice visibility. Browser verification confirmed the page works with real backend data, is strictly read-only, and fires no PUT/PATCH requests. Two minor non-blocking issues noted in test precision but nothing that affects correctness.
